name: Fast Food
description: American and global fast food chains, founders, iconic menu items, and slogans

questions.q: Which fast food chain's signature burger is the Whopper?

source.notes: Introduced in 1957, flame-grilled.

questions.q: What is the name of McDonald's iconic red-haired clown mascot?

source.notes: Debuted in 1963, portrayed first by Willard Scott.

questions.q: Which fast food chain's bacon-loaded signature burger is the Baconator?

source.notes: Launched in 2007; six strips of bacon and two beef patties.

questions.q: 'Eat Fresh' is the long-running advertising slogan of which chain?

source.notes: Used from the late 1990s into the 2020s.

questions.q: Which chain sells the Crunchwrap Supreme and the Doritos Locos Tacos?

source.notes: Doritos Locos Tacos launched 2012 with Frito-Lay.

questions.q: Which chain's cows famously advertise 'Eat Mor Chikin'?

source.notes: Campaign launched 1995 by Atlanta agency The Richards Group.

questions.q: Whose face appears on every bucket of KFC chicken, honoring the chain's founder?

source.notes: Honorary Kentucky Colonel title; secret recipe of 11 herbs and spices.

questions.q: What were the first names of the two brothers who founded the original McDonald's in San Bernardino in 1940?

source.notes: Ray Kroc joined in 1955 and later bought them out.

questions.q: Who founded Wendy's and appeared in over 800 of its TV commercials until his death in 2002?

source.notes: Named the chain after his daughter Melinda Lou 'Wendy' Thomas.

questions.q: Which chain's Double-Double burger and 'Animal Style' secret menu are iconic West Coast staples?

source.notes: Founded 1948 by Harry and Esther Snyder in Baldwin Park, CA.

questions.q: Subway's founders originally opened their first shop in 1965 under what name?

source.notes: Renamed Subway in 1968; founded by Fred DeLuca and Peter Buck.

questions.q: In what year did McDonald's introduce the Big Mac nationally?

source.notes: Invented in 1967 by Jim Delligatti in Uniontown, PA; nationwide in 1968.

questions.q: Which major U.S. fast food chain is famously closed on Sundays in observance of its founder's faith?

source.notes: Founder S. Truett Cathy established the policy in 1946.

questions.q: Burger King operates under which different brand name in Australia?

source.notes: The 'Burger King' name was already trademarked in Australia when the chain entered.

questions.q: Pizza Hut was founded in 1958 in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Founded by brothers Dan and Frank Carney in Wichita.

questions.q: In 2019, which chain launched the Impossible Whopper, making it the first major nationwide burger chain with a plant-based headliner?

source.notes: Partnered with Impossible Foods; rolled out nationwide August 2019.

questions.q: Who founded Taco Bell in 1962?

source.notes: First Taco Bell opened in Downey, California.

questions.q: Which McDonald's breakfast sandwich, introduced in 1972, was invented by franchisee Herb Peterson?

source.notes: Modeled on eggs Benedict; first sold in Santa Barbara, CA.

questions.q: In what year did White Castle — widely considered America's first fast food burger chain — open?

source.notes: Founded in Wichita, Kansas by Walter Anderson and Billy Ingram.

questions.q: In what year did McDonald's introduce the Happy Meal with its signature toy?

source.notes: Initial theme was 'Circus Wagon'.

questions.q: Dairy Queen opened its first store in 1940 in which U.S. state?

source.notes: First store opened in Joliet, Illinois on June 22, 1940.

questions.q: Burger King co-founders James McLamore and David Edgerton opened the first Burger King in 1954 in which U.S. city?

source.notes: Originally named 'Insta-Burger King'; renamed after a 1959 rebranding.
